summary: Canadian artist, heavily inspired by the Indigenous peoples of British Columbia. Known for painting totem poles, forests, and landscapes of the Pacific Northwest. Associate of the Group of Seven (particularly Lawren Harris). Also a writer — her memoir Klee Wyck won the Governor General's Award. Became widely recognized only late in life (age 57). Went by the nickname "Klee Wyck" ("Laughing One").

works.name: Indian Church / Church at Yuquot Village (1929)
works.description: Her most reproduced painting. A white church surrounded by towering green forest. Renamed by the Art Gallery of Ontario in 2018. Wikipedia

images.caption: Indian Church / Church at Yuquot Village (1929)

clues.clue: A white church completely surrounded by greenery/foliage
examples: A white church is completely surrounded by greenery in this artist's painting
This artist's arguably most famous piece depicts a white building surrounded by foliage.

clues.clue: Controversially renamed from "Indian Church" to "Church at Yuquot Village" by the Art Gallery of Ontario in 2018
examples: controversially renamed Church at Yuquot Village by the Art Gallery of Ontario in 2018
That piece's name has recently been changed.

clues.clue: Lawren Harris called it Carr's best work; bought it for his dining room
examples: Lawren Harris bought the painting to showcase it in his dining room, and called it Carr's best work.

works.name: Totem Pole Paintings (Big Raven, Big Eagle, Vanquished, etc.)
works.description: Carr's most iconic subject matter — totem poles in wilderness settings, often depicting Haida and other First Nations sites. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Totem poles in the wilderness were a common subject
examples: Totem poles in the wilderness were a common subject for this artist
This artist painted several depictions of totem poles after visiting Alaska and Haida Gwaii

clues.clue: Big Raven and Big Eagle — paintings of large birds in forests
examples: this painter, who painted depictions of large birds in forests, including Big Raven and Big Eagle

clues.clue: Vanquished — tree stumps under dark clouds; depicts abandoned First Nations village at Skedons Bay
examples: painted several works of only trees, and one of several tree stumps under dark clouds. That painting was "Vanquished."

clues.clue: Visited Alaska and Haida Gwaii to paint these subjects
examples: painted several depictions of totem poles after visiting Alaska and Haida Gwaii

clues.clue: Sonny Assu (Kwakwaka'wakw artist) made digital reinterpretations of Carr paintings with Pop Art elements
examples: An artist of this ethnicity included What a Great Spot for a Walmart! among a series of spaceship-inspired digital reinterpretations of Emily Carr paintings.

works.name: The Crazy Stair / The Crooked Staircase (1928)
works.description: Features a totem figure and a crooked staircase leading to a white building with a large bird on top. Sold for $3.39 million at auction. View painting (CBC)

clues.clue: Depicts a crooked structure/staircase leading up to a white building with a large bird on top
examples: This artist depicted the title crooked structure leading up to a white building with a large bird on top of it in the painting The Crazy Stair.

works.name: Klee Wyck (1941 memoir)
works.description: Memoir of her experiences among First Nations peoples on BC's west coast. Won the Governor General's Award. "Klee Wyck" means "Laughing One" — a nickname given to Carr by the Ucluelet people.

clues.clue: Passages critical of missionaries were removed from the 1951 edition and not restored until 2004
examples: Passages critical of missionaries were removed from the 1951 edition of this artist's memoir Klee Wyck, and were not restored until 2004.

clues.clue: Carr also went by the name Klee Wyck
examples: This painter studied in France under Henry Gibbs, and also went by the name Klee Wyck.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: How Carr is identified and biographical clues.

clues.clue: Canadian artist, famous for depictions of British Columbia
examples: what Canadian, famous for her depictions of British Columbia?

clues.clue: Associate / unofficial member of the Group of Seven; supported by Lawren Harris
examples: supported by Lawren Harris and other landscape painters from her home country
Emily Carr was told by this group of Canadian landscape painters that she was "one of [them]."

clues.clue: Became widely recognized only at the age of 57
examples: This painter, who became widely recognized only at the age of 57

clues.clue: Studied in France under Henry Gibbs
examples: This painter studied in France under Henry Gibbs

clues.clue: Known for pushing around a pram filled with animals, notably a Javanese macaque named Woo
examples: This artist was known for pushing around a pram filled with animals, most notably a Javanese macaque named Woo.

clues.clue: Honored on Canadian postal stamp in 1971
examples: She was honored on her home country's postal stamp in 1971.

clues.clue: Befriended Georgia O'Keeffe in New York
examples: Emily Carr, an occasional but not official member of the Group of Seven, befriended this American artist in New York.

recursive_suggestions: Group of Seven — frequently linked; Carr is an associate member
Lawren Harris — Carr's main supporter within the Group of Seven
Haida Gwaii — appeared as its own answerline (2025 Chicago Open) with Carr mention
